title: Feature/batch norm
body:
Added a boolean `use_batch_norm` flag to the CNN and MLP feature extractors.  
- In **NatureCNN**, each convolutional block now conditionally inserts an `nn.BatchNorm2d` layer (before the ReLU) when `use_batch_norm=True`.  
- In **create_mlp**, an initial `nn.BatchNorm1d` may be applied to the inputs, and batch-norm modules can wrap each linear layer.  
All defaults remain unchanged (batch norm off), and existing behavior is fully preserved.  

## Motivation and Context
Batch normalization often improves training stability and speeds up convergence.  
Enabling `use_batch_norm` yields better PPO performance on benchmark tasks (e.g., faster Breakout learning).  
By making it optional, users get the benefit without breaking existing workflows.  
- [x] I have raised an issue to propose this change ([required](https://github.com/DLR-RM/stable-baselines3/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) for new features and bug fixes)

step_plan.summary: Introduce an optional use_batch_norm flag to NatureCNN and create_mlp that enables BatchNorm2d for CNN blocks and BatchNorm1d for MLP layers, with MlpExtractor propagating the flag. All defaults remain off to preserve backward compatibility; tests should confirm shapes and BN presence when enabled.

steps.operation: Add use_batch_norm parameter to NatureCNN.__init__ and configure CNN blocks to optionally include BatchNorm2d after Conv2d blocks
steps.reason: Introduce an optional BN path so CNN blocks can normalize activations; default remains off to preserve backward compatibility; lays groundwork for BN-enabled CNNs without changing existing behavior when disabled.
steps.side_effects: May require downstream tests to confirm BN presence when enabled; ensures BN modules are properly registered for parameter updates and training/evaluation mode handling.

steps.operation: Modify NatureCNN.forward to apply BatchNorm2d layers in forward pass when present
steps.reason: Ensure that BN layers, when added in __init__, participate correctly in the forward computation and maintain the expected output shape and values.
steps.side_effects: BN layers can affect training dynamics; need to verify outputs are unchanged when BN is disabled and shapes match when BN is enabled.

steps.operation: Extend create_mlp to accept use_batch_norm flag and implement optional initial BatchNorm1d and per-layer BatchNorm1d wrapping
steps.reason: Provide BN support for MLP feature extractors by optionally normalizing inputs and wrapping Linear layers with BatchNorm1d; preserves pre_linear_modules/post_linear_modules hooks; defaults off for backward compatibility.
steps.side_effects: BN changes may affect training behavior; shapes must remain invariant; downstream components consuming MLP outputs should be unaffected when BN is disabled.

steps.operation: Update MlpExtractor.__init__ to propagate use_batch_norm to create_mlp calls
steps.reason: Coordinate BN usage across MLP construction by ensuring the extractor passes use_batch_norm to create_mlp so policy/value nets can leverage BN when requested.
steps.side_effects: If not propagated, BN capability may be missed; ensure compatibility with existing extractor construction paths and default behavior.
